response.justification: Candidate is an experienced senior engineer with strong polyglot background (D, TypeScript, Node.js, Java, C#) and some GIS exposure via GISCollective and Green Map work, which aligns with the geospatial domain. However, the role explicitly requires modern C++ (or equivalent Rust) as the primary technology, and the resume shows only a brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments back in 2014-2015 with no recent C++ or any Rust experience. The distributed-systems and low-latency background at Sociomantic is relevant, but the core language gap is significant for a Senior SDE C++ tiling role.
strengths: Geospatial/GIS domain experience through GISCollective and Green Map projects
Low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems work at Sociomantic (millions of req/s)
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, testability, Linux tooling
concerns: Minimal and dated C++ experience (~6 months in 2014-2015); no Rust experience
No demonstrated work on routing/navigation pipelines or map tiling specifically
Recent stack is JS/TS/Node-heavy, far from the systems-programming focus of the role
